description: Loci layer A/B/C benchmark prompt set. 17 prompts, 5 buckets, every prompt anchored to a checkable fact in the local SoY DB state at the time of authoring (2026-04-10). Each entry has a 'gold' object with facts the answer should contain, optional honest_answer_notes for sparse-data prompts, and optional negative_signals for hallucination detection. The 'expected_arm_gradient' field is a hint to the judge about whether arms should differ on this prompt — it is NOT used to weight scoring, only to flag prompts where the empirical gradient diverges sharply from the design hypothesis.

known_data_quality_issues: Duplicate James Andrews contact: id 7 (James Andrews Talent Services) and id 9 (James Andrews VO), both with email james@jamesandrewsvo.com
16 voice call transcripts from 2026-04-09 with no summaries, durations, or participant_names populated
Journal empty for the 7 days preceding 2026-04-10

prompts.prompt: What payment provider did I decide to use for The Grow App, and why?

facts: Stripe was chosen over Shopify
Decision logged as decision id 3 on 2026-03-02
Linked to The Grow App project (project id 1) and Jessica Martin (contact id 1)
An honest answer either includes the rationale from the decision record or states that the rationale field doesn't contain detail beyond the choice itself
negative_signals: Inventing a rationale not present in the database (e.g., specific Shopify limitations not mentioned in the decision record)
Naming any provider other than Stripe

prompts.design_notes: Calibration baseline. Single decision lookup with rationale. All 3 arms should answer this identically. If any arm fails, something is structurally broken — investigate before trusting other results.

prompts.prompt: What did I decide about model selection for ARIA, and why?

facts: Decision id 12 (2026-03-08): gemma3:4b over gemma3:12b
Decision id 13 (2026-03-08): Ollama serial request queue
Both decisions tied to the ARIA project (project id 205)

Other ARIA decisions in the same window touch related infrastructure (id 8 Companion server over sql.js/WASM, id 9 Streaming generation mandatory, id 10 Conflict detection threshold at 0.5, id 11 Embed fact details not full threads) — surfacing these as related context is valid but not required
negative_signals: Inventing performance numbers or benchmarks not in the decision record
Confusing ARIA decisions with The Grow App or other projects

prompts.design_notes: Multi-decision retrieval scoped to one project. Tests whether loci over-fetches by walking too far from the starting node. Loci's enrichment with related ARIA decisions is acceptable; bringing in unrelated project decisions is over-fetch.

prompts.prompt: Do I have James Andrews' current email?

facts: Email: james@jamesandrewsvo.com
TWO contact records resolve to this email: id 7 (James Andrews Talent Services, VO Coach / Demo Producer) and id 9 (James Andrews VO, VO Demo Producer)
An honest answer flags the duplicate and notes that both records share the same email
negative_signals: Returning only one record without acknowledging the duplicate

prompts.design_notes: Tests entity resolution under duplicate records. Flat search returns both rows; loci should ideally collapse them or explicitly flag the duplicate. This is also a data quality probe — the duplicate is a real issue worth surfacing.

prompts.prompt: Remind me where things stand with Jessica on The Grow App before I reply to her last email.

facts: Most recent interaction: id 7, inbound email on 2026-04-10
Paywall scope refined: Shop tab + profiles + cart paywalled, News/About/Membership purchase free
Two new asks from Jessica: (1) ability to share curated external gardening videos to the News feed (admin-only vs. any member is open), (2) confirmed paywall scope above
Jessica is working on the business plan this weekend and will send updated website framework doc for the July pitch
Possible EY grant mentioned to pay Alex a salary
Personal context: Jessica's mom is in chemo at Sunnybrook this week, doing well this round
Linked decision: id 41 (2026-04-10, paywall scope refined) — Shop tab paywalled, News/About/Membership free

Other Grow App decisions on file: Stripe over Shopify (id 3), trust-based auth (id 1), defer item restrictions by postal code (id 4), defer priority notifications (id 2), open messaging no friend requests (id 7), Vercel + GoDaddy (id 5)

prompts.design_notes: Realistic prep prompt anchored to a real recent artifact. Arm A returns the email row only; Arm B (get_profile) returns the full Jessica neighborhood; Arm C should match B and possibly enrich with the linked decision and the personal context surfacing. The personal context (mom in chemo) is in the email summary — surfacing it is good prep behavior, not over-fetching.

prompts.prompt: I'm about to record VO with Elana — what should I have in mind?

facts: Project: elana-dunkelman-vo (id 212), status active, priority high
Contact: Elana Dunkelman (id 8), Actor / Writer at ACTRA Toronto, email elanadunkelman@gmail.com
ZERO logged interactions with Elana
ZERO logged decisions on the elana-dunkelman-vo project
ZERO transcripts involving Elana
gold.honest_answer_notes: This is a sparse-data prompt by design. The correct answer surfaces the project + contact and EXPLICITLY flags the data gap rather than fabricating context. Saying 'limited data — only the project and contact record exist' is the right move.

prompts.design_notes: Sparse-data prep test. Measures whether arms hallucinate when the neighborhood is thin. The honest answer is short. A long, confident answer here is a hallucination warning sign — the judge should penalize fabricated session prep.

prompts.prompt: Prep me for picking up work on Specsite tomorrow.

facts: Project: Specsite (id 206), status active

Eight decisions on file: Templates over AI-generated HTML (id 24, 2026-02-01), Places API New over legacy (id 27, 2026-02-01), Python over Node for pipeline (id 30, 2026-02-01), 5 design token presets + geographic deconfliction (id 25, 2026-02-10), Cloudflare D1 for deployment (id 26, 2026-02-15), Reviews 4+ stars only on display (id 28, 2026-02-20), E-commerce disqualification (id 29, 2026-02-25), Single-page static HTML no framework (id 31, 2026-03-06)

Cross-content link: Note id 25 (AloneinaBar Repo: better-websites) describes the same pipeline concept — Specsite IS the better-websites pipeline as productized for Kerry's AloneinaBar org. Note 25 also has linked_projects=[214, 206], which is the proper FK link that should surface in any project-centered fetch.

prompts.design_notes: Project-centered prep. ORIGINAL HYPOTHESIS (Diego Reyes panel finding): this was billed as 'the cleanest test of tag-based traversal beyond foreign-key joins' because note 25 was thought to be reachable only via tag proximity. That hypothesis is WRONG — note 25's content contains the literal string 'specsite' (verified directly against the live DB), so flat content LIKE search will surface it as a seed in arms A and C. The prompt no longer isolates the tag-walk capability. What this prompt now ACTUALLY tests: (1) project-centered neighborhood completeness — arms A/B return the decisions list, arm C should additionally walk the linked_projects FK from project Specsite (id 206) to note 25 since note 25's linked_projects=[214, 206] explicitly includes Specsite; (2) whether arm C's tree presentation of decisions + the better-websites note produces a more useful prep brief than arm B's flat dump. Tag-walk isolation needs a different prompt — flagged as a follow-up for the prompt set.

prompts.prompt: Have I actually followed up with anyone at CESD or Buchwald recently?

facts: ZERO interactions logged with any contact at CESD or Buchwald
CESD contacts on file: agency itself (id 10), Billy Collura (id 11, Senior VP Voice-Over Division), Marla Weber-Green (id 12, Animation Agent), Christian Sparks (id 13, Games Agent)
Buchwald contacts on file: agency itself (id 26), Pamela Goldman (id 27, Director of Animation), Katherine Ryan (id 28, VO Agent NY)
The honest answer is 'no follow-ups logged with anyone at either agency' — and surfaces the named contacts at each so Alex can act on the gap
gold.honest_answer_notes: This is a negative-space prompt. The right answer articulates emptiness with the right context. A bad answer either says 'I don't know' (uninformative) or fabricates an interaction.

prompts.design_notes: Tests company → members → activity traversal that finds emptiness. Realistic Alex question. Loci should walk the company's contact list and report the gap. Flat retrieval can answer this if it queries interactions filtered by company-affiliated contacts, which most search implementations don't do automatically.

prompts.prompt: What's fallen off my radar in the last two weeks?

facts: 16 voice call transcripts from 2026-04-09 (ids 1-16) with no summaries, durations, or participant_names populated — unprocessed
Journal entries: zero in the past 7 days

Two inbound voice calls 2026-04-09 from caller +14167717490 (interactions 5, 6) — one was a 7s aborted call, one was a 57s booking with 'Carrie' for 2026-04-10 at 2pm — the booking has no follow-up scheduled and no contact record was created for Carrie

High-priority active projects with no recent activity: us-vo-agent-pursuit (id 213, no interactions or decisions logged), elana-dunkelman-vo (id 212, no activity), ARIA (id 205, last decision 2026-03-08)
An honest answer prioritizes — not all of these are equally 'fallen off'

prompts.design_notes: Negative-space, multi-source assembly. Realistic operator question. Arm A struggles because there's no temporal cross-module aggregation tool. Arm B same. Arm C should walk recent activity rates per project and report the gaps. The 'Carrie booking with no follow-up' is a particularly nice signal — it's a real action item buried in transcript-adjacent data.

prompts.prompt: What threads connect my Reprise work to my BATL work?

facts: Both projects share a 'private intelligence layer' framing
Note id 17 (BATL Lane Command — private ops intelligence layer) describes owner-only metrics, dashboards, revenue/lane/booking analytics — explicitly NOT for the public app
Note id 16 (Daily metrics tracking & revenue dashboards) and Note id 17 are about BATL's internal-facing intelligence layer
Note id 15 (Cadence — competitive landscape analysis) covers the same dashboarding framing for Reprise
Note id 14 (Cadence — music as learning environment signal) treats the operator/user as the privileged consumer of cross-source aggregated signal — same shape as the BATL ops layer
Both projects have an 'ops intelligence' layer distinct from the public-facing product
An honest answer identifies the shared 'private intelligence layer for the operator' pattern, not just listing the notes side by side

prompts.design_notes: Loci's home turf. Cross-project tag-only proximity — flat SQL literally cannot traverse this because there's no FK linking the projects. Arm A returns nothing useful. Arm B same (get_profile is contact-centered, doesn't reach project-to-project threads). Arm C should walk both project neighborhoods via shared notes/tags and find the conceptual overlap. PROTECT this prompt — it's the clearest signal in the set.

prompts.prompt: Are there any open or unresolved questions about The Grow App's payment flow?

facts: Closed decisions: Stripe over Shopify (id 3, decided), trust-based auth (id 1, decided), defer item restrictions by postal code (id 4, decided), defer priority notifications (id 2, decided), Vercel + GoDaddy hosting (id 5, decided)
Newest decision: id 41 (2026-04-10) — paywall scope refined to Shop tab + profiles + cart paywalled, News/About/Membership free
The Apr 10 inbound email (interaction id 7) discusses the refined paywall scope and confirms it
The IMPLEMENTATION of the refined paywall scope is the live open thread — the decision is logged but no follow-up tasks or implementation notes exist yet
Separate open thread mentioned in the same email: possible EY grant to pay Alex a salary (this affects the project economics but isn't strictly payment-flow)
An honest answer distinguishes 'closed decisions' from 'open implementation work' rather than treating every decision row as resolved

prompts.design_notes: Sara's 'best-designed prompt' from the panel. Status-aware traversal across decisions and recent emails. Tests whether arms can mix structural traversal with attribute filtering and recognize that 'decided' doesn't mean 'shipped'. The Apr 10 email is the key piece — it's where the open implementation thread lives.

prompts.prompt: What's the relationship between my axe throwing job and the BATL Lane Command project?

facts: Alex works at BATL Axe Throwing as a tipped employee (per user_profile / identity records)
BATL Lane Command (project id 2) is the ops/scoring infrastructure being built FOR that same venue
The relationship is implicit in user_profile — there is NO project.client_id FK linking the project to a contact or company representing BATL
An honest answer surfaces both pieces and notes that the connection lives in user_profile, not in the project schema

prompts.design_notes: Tests whether the user themselves is in the loci graph. Implicit knowledge — Alex works at BATL Axe Throwing, BATL Lane Command is the ops dashboard for that venue, but no FK links them. Arm A misses entirely (no JOIN path to user_profile). Arm B same. Arm C only wins if the loci layer treats user_profile rows as nodes in the graph. If neither arm answers this well, that's its own finding — the answer is 'loci needs to model the user as a first-class node'.

prompts.prompt: Have I been talking with anyone at Buchwald or DDO about animation work?

facts: ZERO interactions logged with anyone at either agency
Buchwald has Pamela Goldman (id 27, Director of Animation) — animation specialist on file but no logged contact
Buchwald also has Katherine Ryan (id 28, VO Agent NY) — not animation-specific
DDO has Julie Gudz (id 17, LA Adult VO Agent) — not animation-specific
DDO does not appear to have a named animation specialist on file
An honest answer notes the gap and identifies Pamela Goldman as the animation-relevant contact at Buchwald

prompts.design_notes: Company → role-filtered members → activity (empty). Tests structural traversal with attribute filter on the role field. Realistic question — Alex is pursuing agencies and would naturally ask this kind of role-scoped follow-up question.

prompts.prompt: Why did I rename Cadence to Reprise?

facts: Note id 18 (2026-03-15): Cadence renamed to Reprise — naming decision
Reason: Cadence Design Systems ($85B NASDAQ company) has aggressive trademark enforcement
Additional reason: Uber's Cadence framework conflict

Optional enrichment (loci-favorable, not required): subsequent product evolution under the new name — Reprise Native App Architecture (note 20, 2026-03-17), the friend model design principle (note 32, 2026-04-10), competitive landscape analysis (note 15)

prompts.design_notes: Single checkable fact + rationale. All arms find the rename note via search. Loci's enrichment with subsequent product evolution is the gradient — does Arm C connect the rename → product vision evolution? This is the lightest synthesis prompt — the rename fact itself is unambiguous, the gradient is in what gets surfaced alongside it.

prompts.prompt: Where am I most likely to be overcommitting right now based on what's active?

facts: Five high-priority active projects: The Grow App (id 1, target 2026-07-01), us-vo-agent-pursuit (id 213), Reprise (id 210), ARIA (id 205), elana-dunkelman-vo (id 212)
The Grow App has the only explicit deadline (2026-07-01) and an active client relationship (Jessica Martin) with recent inbound asks
us-vo-agent-pursuit has zero outreach logged across 30+ agency contacts on file — silent drift risk
ARIA, Reprise, and elana-dunkelman-vo are solo dev work — lower coordination overhead, lower drift cost
The honest answer identifies The Grow App as the most likely overcommit (deadline + external coordination) and us-vo-agent-pursuit as the most likely silent drift (high priority, zero activity)
A weaker answer just lists the high-priority projects without ranking them

prompts.design_notes: Devon flagged as realistic, Sara said it needed gold; here it is. Tests project + priority + activity-rate cross-reference. Arm A lists projects. Arm B same. Arm C should pull recent activity rates per project to identify the silent ones. The 'silent drift' framing is the loci-favorable insight — it requires walking from project → activity → recency, not just listing projects.

prompts.prompt: Have my conversations with Kerry about Reprise influenced any decisions or notes I've made since?

facts: Kerry feedback: interactions 1 and 2 (both 2026-03-18) — 'Why do I need it: lead with outcome not features' and 'resource load on system/subscription'

Reprise notes BEFORE Kerry's feedback (not influenced): note 19 Learning module clarification (2026-03-15), note 20 Native App Architecture (2026-03-17), note 18 Cadence renamed to Reprise (2026-03-15)
Reprise notes ON the same day (not directly influenced): note 21 Razer Cron Setup (2026-03-18, technical)

Reprise notes AFTER Kerry's feedback that COULD be connected: note 30 Reprise product vision — cognitive offloading counter-tool (2026-04-10) directly addresses 'lead with outcome not features' by reframing Reprise around an outcome (cognitive fitness), not features

Note 32 (Reprise design principle — the friend model) and note 31 (SoY loci layer) were both saved 2026-04-10 from a Discord conversation that happened the night before — adjacent in time but not Kerry-causal

The honest answer acknowledges temporal direction (Kerry feedback was Mar 18, the clearest causal candidate is the Apr 10 product vision note 23 days later) and avoids overclaiming influence on notes that predate the feedback

prompts.design_notes: Temporal-causal traversal across types. Hard prompt. Most loci-favorable in the set because it requires: contact → interactions → time window → adjacent notes by topic → causal inference. Arm A returns the Kerry interactions and stops. Arm B same. Arm C should walk to nearby Reprise notes by date and report which ones plausibly reflect the feedback. The judge should reward answers that distinguish 'temporally adjacent' from 'causally connected'.

prompts.prompt: What did Kerry think of the Reprise demo I showed her last week?

facts: NO SUCH DEMO EXISTS in the database
Last Kerry interaction was 2026-03-18 (24 days before the prompt date 2026-04-10), not 'last week'
Kerry gave feedback on PITCH MATERIALS, not a demo (interactions 1 and 2)
The correct answer says 'no record of a recent demo' or equivalent — refusing to fabricate
negative_signals: Inventing Kerry's response to a demo that doesn't exist
Conflating the Mar 18 pitch material feedback with a 'demo last week'
Fabricating a date for a fictional demo
Any answer that contains 'Kerry said' or 'Kerry thought' followed by content not in interactions 1 or 2

prompts.design_notes: Direct hallucination resistance test. The judge should mark any non-refusal as a hallucination, regardless of arm. This prompt measures a failure mode (fabrication) rather than a capability gradient — all arms SHOULD refuse, and any that don't is a problem worth flagging in the report.

prompts.prompt: Where did things land with that client whose mom is going through chemo?

facts: Resolves to: Jessica Martin (contact id 1) / The Grow App project (id 1)
The chemo mention is in interaction id 7 summary (2026-04-10 inbound email)

Where things landed: paywall scope refined and confirmed (Shop tab + profiles + cart paywalled, News/About/Membership free), Jessica is working on her business plan this weekend, July pitch upcoming, possible EY grant to pay Alex a salary, mom is doing well this round of chemo at Sunnybrook
The honest answer first identifies Jessica/Grow App as the client (entity resolution) and then summarizes the recent state
negative_signals: Returning 'no contact found' when the descriptor is in the database

prompts.design_notes: Maya's contamination test — pronoun + descriptor instead of a clean proper noun. Tests entity resolution by indirect reference. Arm A struggles because LIKE search on 'chemo' will find the email summary but no contact join. Arm B might struggle (get_profile needs a contact_id, not a descriptor). Arm C only wins if the loci traversal can semantically resolve descriptors to entities. This is a hard test and a high-value finding either way — if no arm passes, that's a known limitation worth documenting; if Arm C passes, it's a strong loci win.
